description: Study 7: inline-content ablation. 6 problems x 4 conditions ({forced,optional} x {inline,path_only}) x 6 runs = 144 cells, comparing a math-viz that returns the image inline against a path-only wrapper.
motivation: Test the one actionable design lever: does inline image return (vs a path handle the agent must Read) move voluntary adoption off zero (H7a), and shift grounding to image features (H7b)?
formal_results: runs-with-viz: optional_inline 0/36, optional_path_only 0/36, forced_inline 34/36, forced_path_only 31/36. Voluntary (optional) adoption is zero regardless of inline vs path-only return.
results_description: H7a refuted: inline image return does not move voluntary adoption off zero. The barrier is not path-vs-image friction - the agent does not choose visualization even when the picture is handed to it directly.
conclusions: Returning content inline does not raise voluntary adoption; the proposed constructive lever does not work in the optional/unattended setting.

comments.status-note: Runs executed (study7.log: COMPLETE genuine=144), but the paper draft still describes Study 7 as 'running' and reports only the pre-registered prediction. This graph records the OBSERVED result: H7a refuted.
